stable.description: Core top-level API with strongest compatibility guarantees.
stable.compatibility: No removals or breaking signature changes without explicit deprecation cycle.

advanced.description: Extended top-level convenience API, often lazy-loaded.
advanced.compatibility: Backward compatible by default; import timing may change with lazy/eager refactors.

legacy.description: Legacy module exports preserved for backward compatibility.
legacy.compatibility: Accessible for compatibility but excluded from star-import contract.

module_only.description: Submodule-oriented access points on the facade.
module_only.compatibility: Importable via explicit module access; not part of __all__ symbol contract.

new_top_level_export: Update this manifest in the same change as code export changes.
Add or update contract/regression tests validating access and import UX.
Document compatibility implications in README and docs when behavior changes.
rules.star_import_contract: Only stable and advanced groups are eligible for __all__ membership.
